page: Amazon Web Services r5b.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. Operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz, the system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. While single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by lower stress-ng single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scores, the instance excels in multi-core and memory-intensive operations. It achieves strong results in multi-core stress-ng benchmarks, Passmark CPU tests, and memory bandwidth tests, with uncached read speeds reaching 219.49 GB/sec. Database operations are highly efficient, demonstrated by strong Passmark database scores and 74.14 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.

The primary tradeoff of the r5b.24xlarge is its modest single-threaded execution speed and poor performance in LLM text generation tasks. However, its massive memory capacity and high multi-threaded throughput make it highly cost-efficient for memory-dense, enterprise-scale workloads. It is well-suited for large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, high-throughput static web serving, and data-intensive multi-threaded applications that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services r5b.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance designed for memory-intensive, multi-threaded enterprise workloads. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor, it features 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) running at 3.1 GHz and 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, but offers a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show a clear tradeoff: single-core performance and LLM text generation are relatively weak, whereas multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations are exceptionally strong, with Redis SET operations reaching 74.14 million operations per second. This profile makes the r5b.24xlarge highly efficient for large-scale databases, in-memory caches, and high-throughput data processing tasks where massive memory capacity and parallel processing are prioritized over single-threaded speed.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5b.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong multi-core performance for large-scale database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5b.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and strong multi-core database performance.
tagline: A high-capacity memory-optimized platform delivering exceptional multi-core throughput and memory bandwidth for enterprise database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores
768.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Optimized for large-scale databases and in-memory Redis caching
Moderate single-core performance and limited LLM text generation speed
